topic: fraction multiplication word problem, reasoning about operations
latex:
Sadie is making smoothies. Each smoothie uses $\frac{1}{8}$ cup of yogurt. How much yogurt does she need to make $17$ smoothies? Complete the sentence below.

To solve, we'll need to $\begin{pmatrix} \text{multiply } 1/8 \text{ by } 17 \\ \text{divide } 17 \text{ by } 1/8 \end{pmatrix}$, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL cups of yogurt} \end{pmatrix}$, but we don't know the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL cups of yogurt} \end{pmatrix}$.
answer_latex: To solve, we'll need to \textbf{multiply 1/8 by 17}, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the \textbf{NUMBER of groups}, but we don't know the \textbf{TOTAL cups of yogurt}.
